focus: BGE hybrid column of the technique matrix: local dense embeddings across Cranfield+Tier1, vector/hybrid evaluation, scope classification.
key_findings: BGE hybrid is the first Universal technique: improves 6/6 superset datasets +9.5% to +64.0%, zero regressions; joins the ARCH-0.5 core candidate (ADL-0006, SE-0005).
Gains largest where lexical rerankers failed: FiQA +64% (PRF -3.3%), ArguAna +35.7% (PRF -6.8%), SCIDOCS +34.6%.
Vector-only reproduces published BGE-base numbers on every dataset (harness correctness anchor).
Coverage-rerank and refined PRF remain domain-conditional (3/6).
reusable_lessons: LES-016: the embedding script spawns a fresh Python process per IPC batch (model reload each time); fine for small corpora but pathological at scale (fiqa 57K docs). Use a single-load direct embedder for large datasets.
LES-017: a 946MB embeddings JSON exceeds Node's ~536MB string limit for JSON.parse; large-dataset vector eval needs a numpy path (cross-validated to <0.001 nDCG vs the JS evaluator).
loc_note: New generic vector/hybrid evaluator (JS + numpy large-dataset path), portfolio classifier, embedding-input prep; capability growth for the cross-domain matrix.
evidence_quality_notes: Dense numbers anchored by reproducing published BGE-base scores; the one oversized dataset (fiqa) evaluated with a numpy evaluator cross-validated against the JS one.
